purpose: Defines the !create_claude slash command — generates a PRESCRIPTIVE CLAUDE.md establishing coding standards, architecture patterns, documentation requirements, and styling guidelines for the target project. Differs fundamentally from !scaffold (which DOCUMENTS what exists); this command PRESCRIBES what should be. Accepts an optional inline prompt with architecture directions, otherwise interactively prompts the user via AskUserQuestion for architecture pattern (MVC/MVVM/Feature/Custom), styling approach (CSS Modules/Tailwind/CSS-in-JS/Plain), and standards priority (Strict/Balanced/Minimal). Falls back to detected-stack best-practice defaults when user declines. Supports --update (merge with existing, preserve user customization) and --force (overwrite with confirmation) flags. Refuses to overwrite existing CLAUDE.md without an explicit flag.
gotchas: Differentiation is critical: !scaffold documents what IS; !create_claude prescribes what SHOULD BE. If both run, !create_claude takes precedence as actionable standards.
Never overwrite existing CLAUDE.md without --update or --force flag — existing files represent user work.
Read ONLY config/structure/READMEs/linter configs — do NOT deep-read source files. Infer from tooling, not implementation.
Make CONCRETE decisions: 'Use good naming' is unacceptable; 'Use camelCase for functions, PascalCase for components' is required.
Respect existing patterns when project already has established conventions visible from configs — do not impose conflicting rules.
When user gives no/vague directions, auto-detect the stack and apply best-practice defaults — do not leave TODOs for style choices.
Opinionated > permissive: when in doubt, pick the stricter convention; easier to relax than to add later.
patterns: Three-question interactive setup: architecture / styling / standards level.
Stack-adaptive defaults: detected technologies drive specific guideline choices.
Diff-and-merge for --update mode: preserve customized sections, replace scaffold-marked sections, add new sections.
conventions: Two-flag mode system: --update (merge) and --force (overwrite with confirm).
Templated CLAUDE.md sections: Tech Stack → Architecture → Coding Standards → Styling → Documentation → Testing → Git → Commands → Environment.
